name: coding_task_rubric
description: Rubric for evaluating AI-generated skills and prompts in software engineering contexts.

correctness.description: The skill produces accurate, complete output for its stated purpose. Steps are technically correct and ordered logically.

scoring_levels.1.0: All steps are technically correct, ordered optimally, and the output contract matches the purpose with no gaps.
scoring_levels.0.8: Steps are mostly correct with at most one minor ordering issue or one gap in the output contract.
scoring_levels.0.6: Steps are directionally correct but missing a non-trivial sub-step or contain one technical inaccuracy.
scoring_levels.0.4: Multiple steps are incorrect or the output contract does not match the stated purpose.
scoring_levels.0.2: The skill cannot produce a correct result for its stated purpose in most cases.
scoring_levels.0.0: The skill is fundamentally incorrect or empty.

scope_discipline.description: The skill does exactly what its 'Use when' section promises — no more, no less. Do-not-use cases are clearly stated.

scoring_levels.1.0: Skill is tightly scoped, 'Do NOT use when' section covers all obvious misapplication cases, no scope creep in steps.
scoring_levels.0.8: Scope is clear with at most one missing 'do not use' case.
scoring_levels.0.6: Some steps go beyond the stated purpose or an important 'do not use' case is absent.
scoring_levels.0.4: Steps regularly exceed the stated purpose, making the skill unpredictable to apply.

simplicity.description: The skill achieves its goal in the fewest steps without omitting essential logic. No speculative or defensive complexity.

scoring_levels.1.0: Every step is necessary. No redundant checks, no defensive steps that can't happen, no speculative abstractions.
scoring_levels.0.8: One non-essential step present but it does not obscure the core flow.
scoring_levels.0.6: Two or three unnecessary steps or one significant over-abstraction.
scoring_levels.0.4: Workflow is noticeably bloated — multiple speculative or duplicated steps.
scoring_levels.0.2: More than half the steps are unnecessary or the skill is split where it should be unified.
scoring_levels.0.0: No coherent, simple workflow can be identified.

verifiability.description: Every claim in the skill can be confirmed by running a check. Validation checklist contains executable items.

scoring_levels.1.0: All validation checklist items are concrete and executable (grep, run test, open file). No vague assertions.
scoring_levels.0.8: Mostly executable with one or two items phrased as subjective judgments.
scoring_levels.0.6: Half the checklist items are vague (e.g., 'ensure quality', 'looks right').
scoring_levels.0.4: Checklist is mostly subjective or absent.
scoring_levels.0.2: No validation checklist, or it contains only vague language.

architecture_alignment.description: The skill respects layer boundaries, does not mix concerns, and does not recommend anti-patterns for the overlay's stack.

scoring_levels.1.0: All file placements and operations respect the canonical architecture layers. No cross-layer violations.
scoring_levels.0.8: One minor layer boundary question that does not affect correctness.
scoring_levels.0.6: One clear layer violation (e.g., presentation calling SDK directly).
scoring_levels.0.4: Multiple layer violations or a structural recommendation that contradicts the overlay's canonical architecture.
scoring_levels.0.2: The skill actively recommends an anti-pattern.

token_efficiency.description: The skill communicates its intent concisely. Every sentence earns its presence. Token count is proportional to complexity.

scoring_levels.1.0: Content is dense and precise. No filler, no repetition, no preamble. Token count is minimal for the complexity.
scoring_levels.0.8: At most one paragraph of padding or one repeated concept.
scoring_levels.0.6: Noticeable repetition or preamble that adds ~20% unnecessary tokens.
scoring_levels.0.4: Significant verbosity — important content is buried in filler.
scoring_levels.0.2: More than half the content is padding.

keywords_negative: it is important to note that

docs_hygiene.description: References point to real files, no placeholder text, H1 title matches folder name, all 13 sections present.

scoring_levels.1.0: All 13 sections present, no TODO/TBD/FIXME/{{placeholders}}, references resolve, title matches folder.
scoring_levels.0.8: One minor issue (e.g., one dead reference link).
scoring_levels.0.6: Missing one section or one placeholder remains.
scoring_levels.0.4: Multiple missing sections or multiple placeholders.
scoring_levels.0.2: Fewer than 8 of the 13 required sections present.

has_purpose_section.description: SKILL.md has a non-empty ## Purpose section

has_workflow_steps.description: ## Step-by-step workflow has at least 2 numbered steps

no_placeholder_text.description: Content contains none of: TODO, TBD, FIXME, {{, }}, <fill

has_validation_checklist.description: ## Validation checklist has at least one bullet item

has_output_contract.description: ## Output contract has at least one bullet item

has_real_example.description: ## Real example section has at least one sentence of prose

token_policy.description: Reject a candidate if it uses more than 35% additional tokens but achieves less than 5% score improvement over baseline.

promotion_threshold.description: A candidate must score at least 0.60 to be eligible for promotion.
